channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Seventh Suspect Surrenders in Cavite Student Hazing Case
item.description: Following last week's surrender of six individuals, another member of the Tau Gamma Phi fraternity, Dar Sabilona, has turned himself in to the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group-National Capit...

item.title: Returning to Beirut Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: Journalist Eric Reguly recounts his recent journey back to Beirut amid renewed conflict between Israel and Hezbollah. Previously, in October 2024, Reguly witnessed the devastation in southern Lebanon ...

item.title: Government directs gas supply prioritisation; Meghalaya election violence claims two lives
item.description: The Union government has invoked the Essential Commodities Act, instructing refineries to regulate the production, supply, and distribution of natural gas amid disruptions caused by the West Asia conf...

item.title: China’s First Crewed Lunar Landing Targeted at Rimae Bode by 2030
item.description: China plans to land its first crewed mission on the Moon by the end of 2030, deploying two astronauts via the Lanyue lunar module while a third crew member remains in orbit aboard the Mengzhou-Y space...

item.title: Warriors secure narrow One-Day Cup win over North West Dragons with Peters and De Villiers leading
item.description: In a thrilling Cricket SA One-Day Cup encounter at St George’s Park, the Dafabet Warriors edged out the North West Dragons thanks to standout performances from Gideon Peters and Matthew de Villiers. C...

item.title: Zanzibar to source sand from Mainland Tanzania to support key construction works
item.description: Zanzibar government don announce say wanataka import sand kutoka Mainland Tanzania. This decision come because Zanzibar facing shortage ya sand, especially with all the big development projects in pla...

item.title: Sarah Abo and Husband Cyrus Moran Expect Their First Child
item.description: Sarah Abo, the popular host of the Today Show, has shared the exciting news that she is expecting her first child with her husband, Cyrus Moran. The announcement has been met with congratulations from...

item.title: South Africa faces economic risks as Middle East conflict drives oil prices over $100
item.description: South Africa is becoming increasingly vulnerable to global energy shocks as the ongoing conflict involving Iran, Israel, and the United States drives oil prices above $100 a barrel. The country relies...

item.title: Nottingham murderer exhibited violent behaviour prior to fatal stabbings, inquiry hears
item.description: An inquiry in London has heard that Valdo Calocane, who was later convicted of murdering three people in Nottingham in June 2023, displayed violent behaviour a month prior to the killings. Calocane, d...

item.title: Four Police Officers Remanded in Custody Over Owode Onirin Market Shooting
item.description: For one Ebute Metta Chief Magistrates’ Court, four police officers don find themselves remanded at Kirikiri Custodial Centre after dem dey accused of killing traders for Owode Onirin International Spa...

item.title: Two charred bodies discovered in burned Toyota Wigo in Peñaranda, Nueva Ecija
item.description: In Peñaranda, Nueva Ecija, authorities discovered two charred bodies inside a torched vehicle on Monday morning. The incident occurred in Barangay Sinasajan, where a resident reported a burning motor ...

item.title: Rostam Aziz acquires majority stake in Kenya's Nation Media Group
item.description: Rostam Aziz, prominent Tanzanian businessman with diverse interests across media, mining, telecoms, agriculture, real estate, ports, energy, and construction, has struck a deal to acquire the Nation M...

item.title: Trump inaugurates new oil refinery in Texas with Reliance as key investor
item.description: On 10th March 2026, U.S. President Donald Trump announced the inauguration of a new oil refinery in Brownsville, Texas, naming it the America First Refining project. The President highlighted that the...

item.title: KZN Philharmonic to celebrate Chopin and classical masters in March concert
item.description: The KZN Philharmonic Orchestra will host its second and final 2026 Summer Season concert at the Playhouse Opera on Thursday, 12 March, starting at 7pm. Dutch conductor Conrad van Alphen and Bulgarian ...

item.title: Practical Ways to Develop Science Skills in Preschoolers Through Everyday Activities
item.description: Many parents focus on early reading and maths for their preschoolers but often feel unsure about encouraging science skills at home. Research shows that fostering scientific literacy from a young age ...

item.title: Nova Scotia Government Reverses Decision, Restores $53.6M in Grants Following Public Criticism
item.description: The Nova Scotia government has announced it will reinstate $53.6 million in grants and funding following a public backlash. The decision reverses previous cuts that had drawn significant criticism fro...

item.title: Analysis Shows Interstellar Comet 3I/Atlas Contains High Levels of Methanol
item.description: Comet 3I/Atlas, the third confirmed interstellar object, is leaving the solar system but has provided astronomers with valuable data about its unusual composition. Observations using the ALMA telescop...

item.title: Atletico Madrid thrash Tottenham 5–1 as goalkeeping disaster costs Spurs dearly in Champions League tie
item.description: Atletico Madrid delivered a resounding 5–1 victory over Tottenham Hotspur in the first leg of their Champions League clash at the Metropolitano Stadium. The match was marked by a nightmare performance...

item.title: UP Study Attributes Cebu Flooding to Heavy Rain and Loss of Green Spaces
item.description: A study by the University of the Philippines’ Institute of Environmental Science and Meteorology revealed that Typhoon Tino caused unprecedented flooding in Cebu City due to extreme rainfall and fully...

item.title: Rethinking Global Shipping: The Growing Importance of the Cape Sea Route
item.description: Recent events in the Middle East during February and March 2026 have once again disrupted global shipping flows, highlighting the vulnerability of maritime trade which forms the backbone of the global...
